title: Audit 12 unreviewed simulation engine files — time-travel, what-if, sla-calc, traffic-sim, etc.
description: QA completeness check found 12 simulation files never individually reviewed: architecture-diff.ts, time-travel.ts, sla-calculator.ts, failure-modes.ts, capacity-planner.ts, traffic-simulator.ts, particle-path-cache.ts, report-generator.ts, what-if-engine.ts, pressure-counter-tracker.ts, node-service-rates.ts, simulation-metrics-bus.ts. Read each file, check for bugs, edge cases, and correctness. Focus on: time-travel.ts (does seek work at boundaries?), what-if-engine.ts (does scenario comparison produce correct results?), traffic-simulator.ts (does traffic generation match configured patterns?).
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 files read and reviewed for correctness
Any bugs found converted to individual fix tasks
Dual naming confusion (simulation-metrics-bus.ts vs sim-metrics-bus.ts) investigated and resolved
Report appended to system-design-implementation-audit.md

title: Implement Bottom Panel 'Code' tab — currently a stub with placeholder text
description: QA check found the Code tab in BottomPanel shows placeholder text only. This tab should display: (1) generated code for the current architecture (e.g., Docker Compose, Terraform, K8s YAML), (2) syntax-highlighted with the editor-store language selection, (3) copy-to-clipboard button. Alternatively, show pseudocode/DSL representation of the architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: Code tab displays generated code for current canvas architecture
Code is syntax-highlighted using editor-store language
Copy-to-clipboard button works
Language selector allows switching between formats
No more placeholder text

title: Review and task post-simulation report generator (report-generator.ts)
description: QA check found report-generator.ts exists but was never reviewed in any audit. This file generates post-simulation markdown reports with incident timeline, root cause analysis, and recommendations. Review for: (1) correctness of report content, (2) whether it's wired to the UI (PostSimulationReport.tsx), (3) quality of generated recommendations, (4) whether it surfaces in the bottom panel Report tab.
acceptanceCriteria: report-generator.ts reviewed for correctness
Report tab in bottom panel verified working
Generated report content is educational and accurate
Any bugs found converted to fix tasks

title: Expand topology rule profiles from ~80 to 200+ (close gap with PaperDraw's 741)
description: PaperDraw has 741 topology-aware rule profiles vs Architex's ~80 in rule-database.ts. Each profile defines how a component BEHAVES based on its upstream/downstream context. Priority expansion: (1) Add profiles for ALL 14 node categories (currently only compute/networking/caching covered well), (2) Add multi-hop propagation rules (A→B→C cascade patterns), (3) Add 23 SimulatesAs behavior modes (same component type behaves differently in different topological contexts). Target: 200+ profiles by end of sprint.
acceptanceCriteria: rule-database.ts contains 200+ topology profiles
All 14 NodeCategory types have at least 10 profiles each
Multi-hop propagation rules cover 3-node cascade patterns
Profiles tested with simulation on 5+ templates

title: Expand issue taxonomy from 52 to 100+ named issue types
description: PaperDraw has 150+ formal issue types vs Architex's 52 in issue-taxonomy.ts. Missing categories: JOB (batch job issues), BATCH (batch processing), EXT (external dependencies — 19 types in PaperDraw), additional INFRA (13 in PaperDraw vs our subset). Add at least 50 more issue types covering: job scheduling failures, batch processing issues, external API failures, advanced network issues, and storage-specific failures.
acceptanceCriteria: issue-taxonomy.ts contains 100+ issue types
New categories: JOB, BATCH, EXT expanded
Each issue has: code, title, severity, cause, impact, recommendation, triggerCounters
New issues referenced by at least 1 topology profile in rule-database.ts

title: Investigate and resolve dual file: simulation-metrics-bus.ts vs sim-metrics-bus.ts
description: QA check found TWO files with similar names: sim-metrics-bus.ts (290 lines, reviewed in audit) and simulation-metrics-bus.ts (unknown, never reviewed). One may be dead code, a duplicate, or a different version. Investigate: which is imported by orchestrator? Is one deprecated? Delete the unused one or document why both exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Determined which file is the active one used by simulation-orchestrator.ts
Dead/duplicate file deleted or explicitly marked as deprecated
No import confusion remains
Single canonical path for metrics bus

title: Test special characters in node labels for XSS/injection safety
description: QA edge case sweep found node labels are never tested with special characters. A user could enter `<script>alert(1)</script>` or `${dangerous}` in a node label. Verify: (1) BaseNode renders labels via React (safe by default) not innerHTML, (2) Export functions (Mermaid, PlantUML, Terraform) properly escape labels, (3) URL encoding (to-url.ts) handles special chars, (4) Properties panel config fields sanitize input.
acceptanceCriteria: Node labels with <script> tags render as text, not executed
Export to Mermaid/PlantUML escapes special chars in labels
URL-encoded designs with special char labels decode correctly
No XSS vector found in label rendering pipeline

title: Add loading skeleton states for template gallery and heavy operations
description: QA edge case sweep found no loading states for template gallery (55 templates load at once) and heavy simulation operations. Add: (1) Skeleton cards in template gallery while loading, (2) Loading indicator when starting simulation (orchestrator initialization), (3) Progress indicator for large exports (PNG of 100+ node canvas). Use existing skeleton pattern from dashboard loading.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: Template gallery shows skeleton cards during load
Simulation start shows brief loading indicator before dashboard appears
Large export operations show progress feedback
All skeletons match dark/light theme

title: Test incognito/no-localStorage graceful degradation
description: QA edge case: in incognito mode, localStorage may throw on write. Zustand persist middleware needs to handle this gracefully. Verify: (1) App loads without errors in incognito, (2) All stores initialize with defaults when persist fails, (3) No uncaught errors from localStorage quota exceeded, (4) User sees the app normally (just without persistence). Test in Chrome incognito + Safari private mode.
acceptanceCriteria: App loads and functions in incognito mode without console errors
Stores fall back to defaults when persistence fails
No 'QuotaExceededError' uncaught exceptions
User experience is normal except progress isn't saved

title: Verify and wire export integrations — PlantUML, Terraform, C4 to toolbar
description: QA check found export files exist (terraform-exporter.ts, c4-exporter.ts, to-plantuml.ts) but unclear if they're wired to the CanvasToolbar export dropdown. Verify each: (1) Does the file import correctly? (2) Is it listed in export-manager.ts? (3) Does the toolbar dropdown include it? (4) Does clicking it actually export? Fix any that are built but not connected to UI.
acceptanceCriteria: PlantUML, Terraform, C4 exports accessible from toolbar dropdown
Each export produces valid output for a test template
Export errors show toast notification (not silent failure)
All export formats listed in export dialog

title: Add 20 unit tests for critical untested paths — drag-drop, simulation lifecycle, exports
description: QA check found test coverage is ~20% with ZERO tests for: drag-and-drop node creation, simulation play/pause/stop lifecycle, chaos injection, template loading, edge creation, undo/redo, any overlay component, keyboard shortcuts, command palette, any export function, any import function. Add 20 critical unit tests covering the highest-risk untested paths. Priority: (1) Simulation lifecycle, (2) Drag-drop node creation, (3) Undo/redo, (4) Template loading, (5) Export JSON round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: 20 new test files/cases added
Simulation lifecycle: play → running, pause → paused, stop → idle
Drag-drop: valid data creates node, invalid data is handled
Undo/redo: state reverts correctly after node addition/deletion
Template loading: JSON parsed correctly, nodes/edges created
Export/import round-trip: JSON export → import produces identical canvas
